title: Taxi Subsidy Scheme (TSS)
text:
Taxi Subsidy Scheme (TSS) - SupportGoWhere
Taxi Subsidy Scheme (TSS)
Share link
For persons with permanent disabilities who are medically certified as unable to take public transport and totally dependent on taxi for travel to school, work or SG Enable-supported training.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Up to 80% means-tested subsidy for taxi travel for the purpose of school, work or employment-related training supported by SG Enable
From July 2026, additional 15% subsidies for Singapore Citizens born in and before 1969
Application will be processed within 15 working days upon complete document submission
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Persons with disabilities can apply for the means-tested subsidy for taxi travel for the purpose of school, work or employment-related training supported by SG Enable. The approved subsidy percentage* applies to the total fare for each trip (which covers additional charges such as booking fees, peak period surcharges and ERP charges).




The subsidy amounts will depend on:

Whether applicants are Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents

Per capita monthly household income (PCHI)




* subjected to means testing and meeting eligibility criteria

Who is this for?

Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents

Have a permanent disability based on any one of the following:

Physical Disability: Requires some assistance with at least 1 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living due to physical impairment

A visual impairment: Moderate visual impairment or worse in the better eye 

Intellectual disability

Autism




Proof of disability must be provided using the Disability Verification Form completed by a relevant registered healthcare professional.

Certified as unable to take public transport and totally dependent on taxi for travel

Currently schooling, working, or attending employment-related training supported by SG Enable

Monthly Per Capita Household Income (PCHI) of $3,600 and below

Annual Value (AV) of residence reflected on NRIC of $21,000 and below for households with no income

Currently not owning any motor vehicles




From July 2026, individuals with monthly per capita household income of $3,601 to $4,800 will also qualify for TSS.

How to apply?

For more information on the application process, please visit the Enabling Guide.

Where can I find help?

View more details

SG Enable hotline: 1800-8585-885

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

contactus@sgenable.sg

Enabling Village  20 Lengkok Bahru  #01-01, Singapore 159053

Scheme last updated 11 Mar 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
